jokes.text: I'm not picky! / Ew, gross! You got egg white in my egg!
jokes.punchline: Immediately complains about egg white touching their egg
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction with economical delivery. The specificity of complaining about egg white in an egg is absurd and character-defining.

jokes.text: These are kids' clothes, Mom! I'm not wearing these! / Me neither!
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about their clothes being for kids
jokes.punchline: The absurdity is compounded when the second kid agrees
jokes.explanation: Simple irony of kids rejecting age-appropriate clothes, elevated by the second kid's agreement

jokes.text: I don't get why my kids are so spoiled. We have tons of money, we give them everything they want, and still totally spoiled!
jokes.setup: Parent wondering why their kids are spoiled
jokes.punchline: Lists exactly why they're spoiled while remaining oblivious
jokes.explanation: Classic wealthy parent obliviousness, well-integrated into Amy's character

jokes.text: Hey, does the Orphanarium allow visitors? You know, so people can feel good about themselves, and also frighten their own children?
jokes.punchline: Admitting the dual purpose of self-aggrandizement and child manipulation
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on performative charity with dark honesty about parental manipulation tactics

jokes.text: Lieutenant Kroker! Report for emergency briefing. / Oh, thank God. I'll be back in a month!
jokes.setup: Kif claiming he wouldn't miss volunteering for the world
jokes.punchline: Immediately relieved to escape when duty calls
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Kif - the relief in his voice contrasts beautifully with his earlier enthusiasm

jokes.text: Is this gonna be my new family? / No, Albert. These folks are here to provide useless, unskilled labor, not adopt you. / Oh, thank God.
jokes.setup: Orphan asking if visitors will be his new family
jokes.punchline: Relief when told they're just volunteers, not potential parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark misdirection - we expect the orphan to be disappointed, but he's relieved. The warden's blunt description adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Ah, so the little ones don't choke. / No, so there's enough to go around.
jokes.setup: Leela assumes grapes are cut for safety
jokes.punchline: Actually cut due to scarcity
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection that reveals the orphanage's poverty through subverted expectations

jokes.text: This soup smells delicious! Can I help serve and eat it, yum-yum? / You've gotta make it first. That's just tap water.
jokes.punchline: It's just tap water
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection revealing the orphanage's lack of resources

jokes.text: Mm, chunky.
jokes.setup: Learning the 'soup' is just tap water
jokes.punchline: Still finds something positive to say about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - finding a way to be positive about terrible tap water

jokes.text: Shall I take this putrid trash out? / That isn't trash. It's the donation bin. / Donors put their trash in it.
jokes.setup: Leela offering to take out what looks like trash
jokes.punchline: It's donations, but donors put trash in it
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that reveals how people treat charity donations

jokes.text: Two-thirds of a basketball for you, a clump of melted army men for you, three stuffed animals with tomato sauce for you, and a tricy-- uh, bicycle for you.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and damaged 'toys' including the slip from tricycle to bicycle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of increasingly pathetic 'toys' with the slip revealing even worse reality

jokes.text: Albert, you get a cookie. / Yum!
jokes.punchline: Albert gets just a cookie and is genuinely excited
jokes.explanation: Albert's genuine enthusiasm for a cookie after others get broken toys shows his low expectations

jokes.text: That's Bandit. He gave me my first rabies.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of getting rabies from a pet
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark humor delivered deadpan - 'first rabies' implies there were more, and it's treated as a fond memory

jokes.text: This is way nicer than my room at home! / Mine has too much furnitures. / And it's all so matchy-matchy.
jokes.setup: Spoiled kids seeing sparse orphan room
jokes.punchline: Complaining their rooms are too nice and coordinated
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how spoiled the kids are - they prefer deprivation to luxury

jokes.text: You want me to kick her?
jokes.setup: Mandy expressing preferences about the room
jokes.punchline: Albert casually offering violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Albert's matter-of-fact offer of violence, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: How'd that happen anyway? / Damien did it. With his brain.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact mention of telekinetic powers
jokes.explanation: Casual supernatural reference delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: My man, Damien!
jokes.punchline: Bender's enthusiastic approval of supernatural abilities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender reaction - he'd naturally approve of someone with supernatural powers

jokes.text: I apologize, kids. That was just horrible! I'll buy you all ponies to make up for it.
jokes.setup: Amy seeing Bender's attempt to help
jokes.punchline: Overreacting by promising ponies for minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy character moment - throwing money at any problem, no matter how small

jokes.text: The raccoon lick'ded some of the p'sghetti sauce off.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of raccoon improving toy by licking sauce off
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about the toy's condition improvement via raccoon intervention

jokes.text: Hi! My name is Bearinara! I like your ear! / I like your big empty forehead.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about empty forehead
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between innocent compliment and inadvertent insult

jokes.text: You're a little green froggy just like my daddy!
jokes.punchline: Implies her father is also green and frog-like
jokes.explanation: Simple alien parentage joke delivered innocently

jokes.text: Mandy! Stop kissing garbage! / But, we're having fun!
jokes.setup: Mother telling daughter to stop kissing garbage
jokes.punchline: Daughter's innocent defense that they're having fun
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between adult concern and child's innocent perspective

jokes.text: You know, Kif, among certain females, I'm better known for my de-briefing.
jokes.punchline: Sexual pun on 'de-briefing'
jokes.explanation: Predictable Zapp sexual innuendo, saved by character consistency

jokes.text: That's why I've been keeping it close to my chest. / Uh... / I-I should get a map like that for Amy.
jokes.setup: Admiral saying she keeps secrets close to her chest
jokes.punchline: Kif's awkward reaction implies the map is on her chest
jokes.explanation: Visual gag implied by dialogue, with Kif's characteristic awkwardness

jokes.text: Important means Earth, Lieutenant. / The very survival of Earth is at stake. / Pfft. Amphibios 9.
jokes.punchline: Zapp's dismissive reaction to Earth being in danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp character moment - dismissive of Earth's importance compared to alien worlds

jokes.text: Are these domestic bath salts? What are we, bankrupt?
jokes.setup: Spoiled kids learning lessons about not being spoiled
jokes.punchline: Immediately complaining about bath salt quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing the kids learned nothing, with specific entitled complaint

jokes.text: Hey! Grab. Hands off my kajigger! It's a present for my whatsit! / Your whatsit's too old to play with kajiggers.
jokes.punchline: Using nonsense words like 'kajigger' and 'whatsit'
jokes.explanation: Absurdist approach to toy craze with made-up words

jokes.text: People collect these things? / Oh God, yes. From the moment I nabbed my first one, I was like... ♪ Gotta collect 'em all! ♪
jokes.punchline: Obvious Pokemon reference with musical delivery
jokes.explanation: Obvious pop culture reference but executed with commitment

jokes.text: There! I collected 'em all. / Oh, honey, that's not even close to 'em all. You've got, like, 195 to go.
jokes.setup: Amy thinking she collected them all after grabbing a few
jokes.punchline: Massive number still remaining
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection about the scope of the collecting obsession

jokes.text: ...much like Bitcoin or tulips.
jokes.punchline: Referencing famous economic bubbles
jokes.explanation: Smart economic satire comparing collectibles to famous bubbles. Bitcoin reference dates the episode.

jokes.text: Since baby mammals have relatively large eyes at birth, we evolved to find that cute, so we'd care for our offspring instead of strangling them. / Clever little bastards.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining evolutionary basis of cuteness
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about strangling offspring and Bender's approval
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist on evolutionary science with perfect Bender reaction

jokes.text: The pinprick pupils are ugly and off-putting. / Turn it off! Turn it off!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining what makes things not cute
jokes.punchline: Everyone's horrified reaction to ugly image
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff with group reaction beat

jokes.text: Our desire to obtain them is beyond our control. / I don't know if it's literally beyond our-- / A CuddleFish is up for auction!
jokes.setup: Professor claiming desire is beyond control, Amy starting to object
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately proves the point by abandoning argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure - Amy literally proves the Professor's point mid-objection

jokes.text: Just sneak in and pretend to be orphans till I get back! Go, go, go!
jokes.setup: Amy needing to leave for auction
jokes.punchline: Abandoning kids with instruction to pretend to be orphans
jokes.explanation: Absurd parenting moment - literally abandoning kids to chase toys

jokes.text: All the money in my children's college funds! / Madam, that bid is more than makes any sense.
jokes.punchline: Amy betting college funds, auctioneer's measured response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of bidding madness with formal auctioneer language

jokes.text: We have More Than Makes Any Sense, More Than Makes Any Sense. Do I hear More Than Makes Any Sense and a Half?
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer using the absurd amount names as actual bid increments
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning the auctioneer's assessment into actual bid amounts

jokes.text: Do I hear Girl, Are You Kidding Me Right Now? / You only live once, Mother. Although it's been a very long once.
jokes.punchline: Mom's existential comment about her long life
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of wordplay with unexpected character depth

jokes.text: And instead of reading 'Cashew CuddleFish,' the tag reads, 'Knockoff Phonyfish.'
jokes.punchline: Incredibly obvious fake name
jokes.explanation: 'Knockoff Phonyfish' is hilariously obvious fake branding. Perfect example of lazy counterfeiting.

jokes.text: Maybe they don't exist! Maybe it's all just some kinda sick, fuzzy-wuzzy game!
jokes.setup: Amy's obsession with finding rare toy
jokes.punchline: Paranoid rant using childish language
jokes.explanation: Good character breakdown with contrast between paranoia and childish language

jokes.text: That stain looks like a moldy bunny. / That one looks like a cockaroach! Oh, it ran away.
jokes.punchline: Second 'stain' was actually a cockroach
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection about orphanage conditions

jokes.text: That one is so dirty. Wouldn't you rather have some nice clean ones? / No, I like my dirty one better. It matches me.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to trade dirty toy for clean ones
jokes.punchline: Sally's self-aware comment about matching the dirty toy
jokes.explanation: Poignant and funny - Sally's self-awareness about her condition with innocent delivery

jokes.text: I have a weird feeling about this, Kif. / Set tension level to maximum.
jokes.punchline: Treating tension as a controllable setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp moment treating emotions like ship controls

jokes.text: It's as if they had their very lives cuddled out of them!
jokes.punchline: Death by excessive cuddling
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - making cuddling sinister and deadly

jokes.text: They just made a harmless delivery and stole some stuff. Nothing illegal about that.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of theft as normal business
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating theft as routine business practice

jokes.text: Any alien invaders around here? / Uh-oh. The fuzz!
jokes.punchline: Treating law enforcement as criminals would
jokes.explanation: Simple role reversal with crew treating military as threats

jokes.text: My God! What? What's in there? / Little plastic beads! Or beans! / What did you expect? It's a beanbag.
jokes.punchline: Amy's matter-of-fact explanation of obvious contents
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment with Amy's practical response

jokes.text: Whoa, this is no bean. It's alive! / Aren't beans alive? / Cork it, nerd!
jokes.punchline: Amy's logical question and Bender's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay with Amy's logic vs Bender's impatience

jokes.text: The entire organism is filled with spores! It's essentially just one big reproductive organ. / Much like certain humans, eh, Leela?
jokes.punchline: Zapp's inappropriate personal comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Zapp inappropriate comment, saved by character consistency

jokes.text: ♪ I got it, I got it! The rarest in the world! By ripping off a little orphan girl! ♪ / I'm a terrible person.
jokes.punchline: Singing about ripping off orphan, then self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Amy's celebration followed by moral realization

jokes.text: This is an invasion where we're buying the invaders and bringing them into our homes! / Like my black mold Chia Pet?
jokes.punchline: Amy's innocent comparison to her dangerous pet
jokes.explanation: Good comparison that reveals Amy's poor judgment in pet choices

jokes.text: It's special to me, 'cause it's the first time a mommy ever gived me a present.
jokes.setup: Amy insisting on taking back valuable toy
jokes.punchline: Sally's innocent explanation of its true value
jokes.explanation: Emotionally resonant moment that highlights the contrast between material and emotional value

jokes.text: I can't do it. It's too adorable! / Don't be ridiculous. Give it to me. / Oh! Dammit! The cuteness is too much, even for me! And I believe Bambi's mother deserved it!
jokes.setup: Characters unable to destroy cute aliens
jokes.punchline: Leela's extreme example of her callousness toward cute things
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark comedy - Leela's extreme callousness reference perfectly establishes how overwhelmingly cute these creatures are

jokes.text: Ah! Those tiny pinprick pupils!
jokes.setup: Earlier explanation of what makes things not cute
jokes.punchline: Using that knowledge to resist cuteness
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback that provides the solution to the problem

jokes.text: Nobody gets to hug Kif to death but me!
jokes.punchline: Claiming exclusive rights to kill him through affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy line - possessive love expressed through dark humor about deadly affection

jokes.text: Wrong! Because to a mother, the cutest thing in the universe is her own child.
jokes.setup: Alpha Funbag claiming to be cutest thing in universe
jokes.punchline: Amy's maternal instinct overcoming artificial cuteness
jokes.explanation: Satisfying character resolution - Amy's growth as a mother provides the key to victory

jokes.text: The Alpha Funbag is dead? How on earth did you do it?! / Cute recognize cute.
jokes.setup: Professor asking how Amy defeated the creature
jokes.punchline: Play on 'game recognize game' with cuteness theme
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay adaptation with perfect economy

jokes.text: They may be lousy toys, but they're a great investment. / And I collected 'em all!
jokes.setup: Learning the toys are now worthless as toys
jokes.punchline: Still celebrating collecting them all despite their uselessness
jokes.explanation: Character consistency showing Amy's continued materialism despite the lesson

jokes.text: ♪ Gotta collect 'em, gotta collect 'em, Gotta collect 'em all! ♪
jokes.punchline: Everyone joining in the collecting obsession
jokes.explanation: Ending callback that reinforces the collecting theme. Functional but not particularly clever.
